text: The attention ceiling becomes felt firsthand when juggling agents across several repos at once.

evidence.quote: When you're juggling agents across several repos at once, you feel the attention ceiling firsthand.

text: I still think it falls apart in practice, because the hierarchy in the UI is lacking, so its actually hard to know where to focus

text: Inboxes are better than nothing but they don't resolve by themselves and require a lot of work to get to inbox zero.

evidence.quote: Inboxes are better than nothing, but we all know they don't resolve by themselves and need a lot of work to get to inbox zero

text: The bottleneck when running multiple agents is our own attention and sensory throughput, meaning we cannot cheat our biology.

evidence.quote: The main realisation I've had is that with running multiple agents is we cannot cheat our biology; the bottleneck is our own attention and sensory throughput.

text: Ambient background automation is the only type of work that fits "set and forget" with agents, where people don't want to do the work and also don't care about having oversight over it.

evidence.quote: I argued a few weeks ago that only one type of work falls into the bucket of "set and forget" with agents - the ambient background automation - where people don't want to do the work, and also don't care about having oversight over it

text: Most of the current and future agent work lives in the quadrant of exceptions and approvals that require human attention.

evidence.quote: Most of the stuff we deal with today with agents lives and will probably continue to live in that top-right quadrant of exceptions and approvals.
